permissions_or_entitlements_required: Requires normal device access and Apple restore eligibility.
Activation Lock, device lock state, internet access, and signing validation still apply.
evidence_source: /Applications/Apple Configurator.app/Contents/Resources/cfgutil.1 documents restore|update [-I path to IPSW], --ecid selection, --format JSON, and --progress.
cfgutil strings include restore, update, revive, custom IPSW path handling, restore options, restore policy, and firmware build lookup.

candidate_backends.purpose: Apple GUI restore/update workflow and Automator actions.
candidate_backends.callable_from_userland: GUI only; not selected as automation backend.
permissions_or_entitlements_required: Uses internal controllers/XPC services not exposed as stable CLI API.
evidence_source: Strings include restoreDevice:, installFirmwareWithURLs:targetedItems:, RestoreOptionsAssistant, AuthInstallRestoreBehavior, and com.apple.MobileDevice.MobileRestore.

candidate_backends.purpose: Private MobileDevice/AuthInstall restore, TSS, personalization, DFU, and service APIs.
permissions_or_entitlements_required: Some paths appear to require privileged USB support, XPC services, or Apple-internal clients.
evidence_source: nm/strings show AMAuthInstall* TSS request APIs, AMRestore/restorable symbols, APNonce/SepNonce strings, and restore service names.

candidate_backends.callable_from_userland: No documented restore CLI contract found locally.
No documented direct restore CLI contract found locally.

candidate_backends.purpose: Open-source comparison tools for device metadata and restore execution.
candidate_backends.execution_supported: idevicerestore is fallback/comparison only; cfgutil is preferred when available.
evidence_source: Existing restorectl uses ideviceinfo and irecovery for dry-run device mode/identifier detection.

candidate_backends.purpose: Factory/internal restore-style tools found on some systems.
permissions_or_entitlements_required: Unknown; may require factory environment, internal credentials, entitlements, or specific device states.
evidence_source: Local PATH inventory only; not reversed into an executor.

device_detection: Use ideviceinfo -x for normal mode when available.
Use irecovery -q for recovery/DFU metadata when available.
Use cfgutil list or cfgutil --ecid as the Apple Configurator equivalent for selection.
ipsw_validation: Report ProductVersion, ProductBuildVersion, SupportedProductTypes, and selected BuildIdentity.
signing_tss_check: Dry-run reports signing as not locally verified.
Execution delegates signing/APTicket/APNonce/SEP/baseband validation to cfgutil or idevicerestore and stops on failure.
erase_update_decision: update means data-preserving update where backend and device state support it.
cfgutil documentation states recovery-mode restore/update erases the device.
restore_handoff: Fallback/comparison: idevicerestore -e <ipsw> for erase, or idevicerestore <ipsw> for non-erase update-style handoff.
No direct MobileDevice/AuthInstall private API calls.
